analysis.core_story_summary: After killing the demons king Ravan, Sri Ram returned to Ayodhya. Many sages had arrived there to bless him on the occasion of his crowning ceremony. Sage Vashishth was one of them.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Sri Ram kills Shambuk — Kids (8-12):** After killing the demons king Ravan, Sri Ram returned to Ayodhya. Many sages had arrived there to bless him on the occasion of his crowning ceremony. Sage Vashishth was one of them. This story teaches dedication and sacrifice. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **Sri Ram kills Shambuk — Teens (13-17):** After killing the demons king Ravan, Sri Ram returned to Ayodhya. Many sages had arrived there to bless him on the occasion of his crowning ceremony. Sage Vashishth was one of them. For teens, the key idea is dedication and sacrifice. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **Sri Ram kills Shambuk — Young Adults:** After killing the demons king Ravan, Sri Ram returned to Ayodhya. Many sages had arrived there to bless him on the occasion of his crowning ceremony. Sage Vashishth was one of them. A modern takeaway is dedication and sacrifice. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **Sri Ram kills Shambuk — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** After killing the demons king Ravan, Sri Ram returned to Ayodhya. Many sages had arrived there to bless him on the occasion of his crowning ceremony. Sage Vashishth was one of them. The episode highlights dedication and sacrifice. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Sri Ram kills Shambuk — Global General Audience:** After killing the demons king Ravan, Sri Ram returned to Ayodhya. Many sages had arrived there to bless him on the occasion of his crowning ceremony. Sage Vashishth was one of them. In universal terms, it explores dedication and sacrifice. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
